channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Oil surges past $100 in a first since May as Middle East conflicts rage
item.description: Oil prices rise for a fifth day after Yemen's Houthis say they struck Saudi Arabian oil tankers in the Red Sea.

item.title: A Game of Double Standards
item.description: Qatar 2022: a World Cup that exposed football’s politics, power struggles, and double standards.

item.title: Trump: US-Saudi nuclear deal hinges on Saudi joining Abraham Accords
item.description: The US and Saudi Arabia have reached a civilian nuclear cooperation deal.

item.title: Lebanon Latest: President Aoun visits White House to discuss Hezbollah
item.description: Al Jazeera’s Zeina Khodr brings you the latest from Beirut.

item.title: Lebanon Latest: President Aoun visits White House to discuss Hezbollah
item.description: Al Jazeera’s Zeina Khodr brings you the latest from Beirut after Lebanon’s President meets with Donald Trump.

item.title: Yemen faces a new challenge amid prospects of confrontation
item.description: What is the military balance of power in Yemen and the likelihood of confrontation between the government and Houthis?

item.title: US House votes to limit Iran war for first time since ceasefire breakdown
item.description: The Senate was also expected to vote on War Powers resolution, in largely symbolic move attempting to reign in Trump.

item.title: The missing half of mediation
item.description: The future of diplomacy between the US and Iran depends on giving mediators the backing needed to make agreements stick.

item.title: Norway Viking Row gets ‘resign’ twist in ‘Cockroach’ protest
item.description: Cockroach Janty Party-led protesters in Mumbai, India recreated Norway’s viral Viking Row, replacing ‘ro’ with ‘resign’.

item.title: Two Palestinians shot dead by Israeli forces as West Bank fields set ablaze
item.description: Two men were killed in the town of Beit Furik, east of Nablus. An Israeli settler was also reportedly stabbed.

item.title: In India, fear has switched sides
item.description: By refusing to be intimidated, India’s students have turned a protest over education into a crisis for Narendra Modi.

item.title: More than 100 UK millionaires ask new PM Andy Burnham to tax them more
item.description: The 'Patriotic Millionaires' say 'wealth and power have been concentrated in a small group for too long'.

item.title: Trump says Saudi nuclear pact hinges on kingdom joining Abraham Accords
item.description: Claim casts further confusion over deal to launch US-supported civilian nuclear programme in Saudi Arabia.

item.title: Kuwait-Iraq border crossing hit in drone attack
item.description: Kuwait's Defence Ministry said a drone attack hit the Abdali border crossing with Iraq, causing damage but no casualties

item.title: How shipping insurance rates are rising, as Hormuz, Bab al-Mandeb shut down
item.description: Insurance rates through Hormuz are currently four times the five-year average.

item.title: Has Russia helped Iran target CIA sites in the Gulf?
item.description: Reports of Iran targeting sites belonging to the US's CIA first emerged in the early days of the Iran war.

item.title: Ebola death toll in DR Congo surpasses 1,000
item.description: More than 1,000 people have died in the latest Ebola outbreak in DR Congo, according to official government data.

item.title: Why do some of the cheapest cities cost the most to live in?
item.description: A worker in Cairo can afford 22 hamburgers a month. In Tel Aviv, more than 200. AJLabs counts a salary in Big Mac meals.

item.title: Kashmiri activist Khurram Parvez and journalist Irfan Mehraj freed on bail
item.description: Indian authorities release the two men after years in prison although both remain under tight court-imposed restrictions

item.title: Rebuilding Gaza to cost $71bn, must include Palestinians, report finds
item.description: Rebuilding the enclave will cost seven times the combined cost of all past offensives, says joint report.

item.title: How are companies, governments responding to the OpenAI hack?
item.description: OpenAI's AI models hacked into another company, prompting calls for scrutiny of safeguards for advanced AI systems.

item.title: ‘Our fight is to fix the system’: India protesters demand accountability
item.description: India's Cockroach Janta Party led weeks of protests over exam paper leaks.

item.title: Which Middle East nations have nuclear programmes, and how were they built?
item.description: Saudi Arabia aims to join the region's nuclear club after it signs a deal with the US.

item.title: Cricket: No Ashes 2027 Tests to take place in the north of England
item.description: England will not host Australia at Old Trafford or Headingley.

item.title: EU hits Google with new $1bn fine, saying it broke digital antitrust rules
item.description: The fine is part of a major crackdown on Big Tech by Brussels which says it is seeking to ensure fair competition.
